hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in the ECL2/TM5 pocket, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/5 binding by avoiding steric clashes in their conserved subpockets. This combination will increase the selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is insufficient to consistently achieve SSTR2 selectivity. We hypothesize that disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions will increase the Δmargin and improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we need to disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
Introducing mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 receptors while preserving the native pharmacophore and cysteine residues.
